issues_classified.description: Proposition 1's geometric prediction (absorbed pairs have SMALLER decoder-decoder angle) is directly falsified by B1_decoder_geometry.json (AUROC=0.318 for cos² classifier; pos_mean=0.052 < neg_mean=0.127, direction reversed). Paper's three-sentence 'post-convergence equilibrium' reframing in Section 3.3 is asserted without derivation. Introduction and Abstract continue to present P1 as empirically predictive without acknowledging falsification. This is a RECURRING issue: iter_001 lessons_learned flagged 'Theorem 1 proof sketch is circular'; iter_002 supervisor flags same falsification.
issues_classified.suggestion: Reorder three contributions: (1) EDA empirical detection first, (2) RD framework as theoretical motivation for when absorption is loss-preferred (NOT a geometric predictor), (3) Proposition 2 as mechanistic conjecture bridging theory and EDA. Add one sentence to Section 3.2: 'Onset geometry (small theta_{p,c}) does not characterize post-convergence state; B1 shows absorbed features have LARGER decoder angle post-convergence (AUROC=0.318 < 0.5).' Cost: 2-3 sentences + contribution reordering. Verify: B1_decoder_geometry.json AUROC=0.318 cited in paper.

issues_classified.description: Cross-directional metric label count discrepancy: paper states 'proxy labels (n_pos=50)' for AUROC=0.730, but B1_pairwise_eda.json uses threshold=0.29 yielding n_pos=63. Three label sets in play (exact n=18, proxy n=50 in D1/D2, proxy n=63 in B1) are not disambiguated in Table 1. Jaccard overlap 0.115 was computed between n=18 and n=50, not n=63. This is a NEW critical issue surfaced in iter_002 supervisor review.
issues_classified.suggestion: Audit chain from B1_pairwise_eda.json (threshold=0.29, n_pos=63) to paper's stated n_pos=50. Add dedicated column header in Table 1 clearly showing label set per row: 'exact n=18 / proxy n=50 / proxy n=63'. Recompute Jaccard overlap if proxy set is n=63. This is a single Python audit + table update. Cost: ~30 min. Verify: Table 1 footnotes explicitly identify which rows use which label set.

issues_classified.description: Practical shortlisting claim 'tractable candidate set for downstream IG-based verification' is directly contradicted by D1_eda_validation.json: Precision@50=0, Precision@100=0, Precision@500=0. Minimum k at which EDA enrichment becomes non-zero is not reported. AUPRC=2.09x base rate (below pre-registration threshold of 3x). This misleading claim appears in Abstract, Section 5.1, and Conclusion. This is a RECURRING issue: iter_001 noted EDA AUPRC insufficient; iter_002 supervisor flags same Precision@k contradiction.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace 'tractable candidate set' with 'statistical enrichment at distributional level (AUROC=0.650, AUPRC=2.09x base rate)'. Report minimum k at which Precision@k > 0 (compute from D1 rank order). Reframe Abstract claim: remove 'first probe-free statistical shortlist method'. Correct framing: 'EDA provides ~9x enrichment vs. exhaustive sweep in the top-1000 candidate pool'. Cost: prose changes only + one Python computation. Verify: Abstract and Conclusion no longer use 'shortlist' or 'tractable candidate set'.

issues_classified.description: EDA_norm (AUROC=0.738) significantly outperforms EDA_base (AUROC=0.650, DeLong p=0.0007) — statistically significant improvement. Paper leads all summary claims with EDA_base, burying EDA_norm as a variant. EDA_norm = EDA_base × ||enc_j|| and its superiority is mechanistically consistent with P2 (absorbed features develop elevated encoder norms under competing gradient pressure). This suppression of a stronger metric introduces systematic downward bias in reported performance. NEW in iter_002.
issues_classified.suggestion: Promote EDA_norm (AUROC=0.738) as primary reported EDA variant in Table 1, Abstract, Introduction, and Conclusion. Report EDA_base (AUROC=0.650) as secondary diagnostic. Add one sentence explaining the mechanistic connection: EDA_norm weights by encoder magnitude, consistent with P2's competing-gradient interpretation. Cost: Table update + prose changes. Verify: Abstract uses AUROC=0.738, not 0.650.

issues_classified.description: Four critical numerical inconsistencies spanning multiple paper locations: (a) Phantom AUROC=0.206 in Introduction and Conclusion (Table 1 shows 0.350 for raw cosine); (b) Absorption rate range: Introduction '92-97%' vs Section 4.4 '0.876-0.978' vs Conclusion '0.919-0.968' — three-way inconsistency; (c) Spearman rho: Introduction ρ=0.191 vs Section 4.4 ρ=-0.482 (all configs) vs ρ=-0.100 (jb only) — different sign and magnitude; (d) Table 1 caption says n_pos=50 for cross-directional while raw data shows n_pos=63. ALL four items are RECURRING from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: (a) Replace AUROC=0.206 with 0.350 everywhere (3 locations). (b) Use '0.876-0.978' uniformly — verify from E1_phase_transition.json; add jb-suite parenthetical '(0.938-0.967 for primary jb suite)' in Section 4.4 only. (c) Choose canonical Spearman rho=−0.482 (E1, absorption_rate vs 1/L0, all 11 configs) for phase stability claim; use consistently in Introduction and Section 4.4; label ρ=0.191 explicitly as EDA_delta vs 1/L0 if retained. (d) See W002. Total cost: find-and-replace + prose. Verify: paper.md contains exactly one value for each statistic with consistent sourcing.

issues_classified.description: Missing Abstract: paper_draft.md begins directly with Introduction. Outline contains a full draft abstract. A paper without an abstract cannot be submitted. Section numbering is inconsistent: Introduction/Related Work unnumbered, Method uses 3.x, Experiments uses '# 4 Experiments' explicit numbering. Roadmap sentence at end of Introduction describes reversed paper flow (says Related Work comes after Method). Figure 1 is referenced twice (line 82 and line 404). All four are RECURRING structural issues from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add abstract from writing/outline.md before Introduction (check for the four numerical inconsistencies in W005 before adding). Standardize section numbering to explicit LaTeX-style (1 Introduction, 2 Related Work, 3 Method, 4 Experiments, 5 Discussion, 6 Conclusion). Fix roadmap sentence to correctly describe linear section order. Remove orphan Figure 1 reference at line 82. Cost: ~1 hour editing. Verify: paper.md compiles without duplicate figures, section numbers match sequentially.

issues_classified.description: ASI (originally proposed probe-free detector) was falsified (AUROC=0.4215, below null mean=0.4973) and is not the paper's primary contribution, yet the paper does not clearly state this discovery path. Reviewers familiar with the original proposal structure may be confused about why ASI fails while EDA succeeds. This reduces scientific transparency and risks reviewer credibility challenges. This is a RECURRING issue: iter_001 flagged EDA=SAEBench metric disclosure; iter_002 flags ASI failure narrative gap.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add one sentence to Section 3.3 or Section 4.1: 'The originally proposed Absorption Susceptibility Index (ASI = cos²(θ_{p,c}) × freq_ratio) failed empirical validation (AUROC=0.4215, below null mean); EDA was identified as the operative geometric signal during exploration.' This is intellectually honest and aligns with the paper's exemplary negative result reporting. Cost: 1 sentence. Verify: Section contains explicit ASI failure statement.

issues_classified.description: B3 cross-architecture comparison (Standard L1 vs TopK) uses different hook points: Standard uses resid_pre at layer 6, TopK uses resid_post at layer 6. This confound is documented in audit_report.json but not acknowledged in the paper. The paper presents TopK's 'weaker EDA signal' as evidence for training regime effects, but hook-point difference is a competing explanation. NEW in iter_002.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add explicit caveat sentence to Section 4.3: 'Note that TopK SAE uses blocks.6.hook_resid_post while Standard SAE uses blocks.6.hook_resid_pre; this hook-point difference is a confound that may contribute to the EDA signal difference independently of sparsity formulation.' Cost: 1 sentence. Verify: Section 4.3 contains confound caveat; no causal claim about sparsity formulation without qualification.

issues_classified.description: Reproducibility gaps: sae-spelling repository commit hash, FeatureAbsorptionCalculator parameters (threshold, n_tokens, letter set), and SAELens release hash are absent from Section 3.4. The 18 absorbed feature IDs are in D1_eda_validation.json (label_source_file: iter_001/exp/results/r4/r4a_direct_labels.json) but not in the paper. Replication with different sae-spelling version could yield different absorbed feature IDs and a different AUROC. RECURRING from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add to Section 3.4: (1) sae-spelling repository URL and commit hash; (2) FeatureAbsorptionCalculator parameters (threshold=0.32 from D2 config, n_tokens=3126, letters evaluated); (3) SAELens release identifier 'gpt2-small-res-jb'; (4) the 18 absorbed feature IDs as an appendix table. Cost: ~30 min fact-gathering from D1/D2 JSON files. Verify: Section 3.4 contains all four items.

issues_classified.description: Encoder norm (AUROC=0.757, AUPRC=5.68x) outperforms EDA_base (AUROC=0.650) and the difference is NOT statistically significant (DeLong p=0.153). Paper treats encoder norm as 'confounded baseline' but does not provide evidence distinguishing absorption-driven norm inflation from polysemanticity-driven norm inflation. The paper cannot claim EDA is more mechanistically interpretable without supporting evidence. NEW in iter_002 (not in iter_001 lessons).
issues_classified.suggestion: Either: (a) accept encoder norm as primary empirical detector and reframe EDA as providing mechanistic directional insight; or (b) provide evidence that EDA separates from encoder norm in polysemanticity-controlled subset (check Neuronpedia or logit-based polysemanticity estimates for the 18 exact positives vs negatives). Note: if EDA_norm is promoted per W004, DeLong comparison should shift to EDA_norm vs encoder_norm. Cost: 1-2 sentences clarification or ~2 hr experiment. Verify: paper does not claim EDA superiority over encoder norm without statistical support.

issues_classified.description: Cross-domain semantic hierarchy experiments (animate_inanimate, noun_proper) show absorption_rate=9.99e-9 (zero) with n=20 words each. Result is critically underpowered: to detect 5% absorption rate above null with 80% power requires n≈500 events. The null result does not support 'semantic absorption is absent in GPT-2 Small' — only that it was not detected with methods and sample sizes used. Furthermore, the animate_inanimate probe achieves F1=1.0 on n_test=22 without shuffle control reported (city_country was excluded after failing shuffle at F1=0.621). RECURRING from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: Restate semantic absorption null result as 'not detected with n=20 words per hierarchy using top-5 probe-aligned parent identification'. Report animate_inanimate shuffle control F1 explicitly. Either increase n_words to ≥100 per hierarchy for a powered null result, or accept current result as underpowered and add power calculation to Section 4.5. Cost: prose restatement (low cost) OR experiment expansion (moderate cost ~1-2 hr). Verify: paper does not claim semantic absorption absence.

issues_classified.description: EDA magnitude tension (mean EDA=0.671 ≈ 48° angle vs. small theta predicted at onset) appears in both Section 4.2 and Section 5.2 in nearly identical language, signaling unresolved handling. Paper presented same tension twice without deciding how to present it. RECURRING from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove brief mention in Section 4.2 ('We report this as an open question'). Retain only the fuller treatment in Section 5.2 with temporal-drift reconciliation hypothesis. Cost: 2-line deletion. Verify: EDA magnitude tension appears exactly once in paper.

issues_classified.description: Hysteresis experiment (Section 4.4) is uninterpretable due to ceiling saturation. Fine-tuned SAE: absorption 0.959 → 0.960 (unchanged). From-scratch: 0.964. Three equally consistent hypotheses (hysteresis, universal absorption in this L0 range, measurement noise) are all consistent with the data. Conclusion claims 'consistent with the absorbed state being metastable' — this is incomplete and not well-supported. RECURRING from iter_001.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace metastability claim in Conclusion with: 'We were unable to test hysteresis due to saturation of absorption rates across the tested L0 range; the saturation itself demonstrates that adjusting L0 within the pre-trained SAE range does not escape the absorbed basin.' Cost: prose change. Verify: Conclusion does not use 'metastable'.

issues_classified.description: Scope limitation: all positive EDA results are GPT-2 Small (117M) only. No Gemma Scope experiments despite being in original proposal. Most NeurIPS reviewers will consider GPT-2 Small-only as minimal proof-of-concept. AJT polarity reversal (all 3 SAEs show EDA_delta < 0) limits the claim to L1-penalized SAEs at mid-layers. Scope acknowledged but limits practical utility claims. RECURRING from iter_001 (Gemma access blocked).
issues_classified.suggestion: Priority path 1: Add one Gemma 2 2B Scope configuration to Table 1 (single layer, single SAE configuration). Even one data point substantially strengthens generalization claim. Priority path 2: If Gemma access still unavailable, add GPT-2 Layer 10 results to Table 1 (exact labels from D1-equivalent computation at L10). Priority path 3: Explicitly scope all claims to 'L1-penalized SAEs at mid-layers of GPT-2 Small'. Cost: moderate experiment (1-2 hr for GPT-2 L10 exact-label EDA) or infrastructure access. Verify: at least one model beyond current primary configuration in Table 1, or explicit scope statement.

issues_classified.description: PIPELINE BLOCKING ISSUE: iter_001 action plan flagged EDA ordering facts error and phantom AUROC numbers as BLOCKING before any further writing. Writing agent in iter_002 proceeded WITHOUT verifying these blocking conditions. The pipeline has no machine-verifiable gate to enforce BLOCKING issues before writing starts. This creates a systemic loop where the same issues recur every iteration. This is a RECURRING pipeline issue.
issues_classified.suggestion: Before launching writing agent in iter_003: add a pre-writing gate that (1) reads action_plan.json, (2) extracts all items with blocking=true, (3) verifies each against paper.md or source JSON, (4) refuses to proceed if any blocking item is unresolved. Implement as a planner pre-check step. The specific blocking items from this iteration are W001, W002, W003, W005. Cost: 1 planning step modification. Verify: writing agent is never launched when blocking=true items exist in current action_plan.

issues_fixed: The tautological EDA variant (AUROC=1.0) is properly documented and excluded from all summary claims — this was flagged in iter_001 and correctly handled in iter_002.
city_country_binary probe excluded from cross-domain analysis after failing shuffle control (F1=0.621 > gate) — correctly applied per iter_001 recommendation.
First-letter cross-domain absorption (Section 4.5): ratio_to_null=10.0 with 120 IG-ablation events correctly calculated and consistent with source data C2_child_suppression_absorption.json.
Bootstrap CIs (10k resamples) and DeLong tests consistently applied throughout — standard was maintained from iter_001.
Statistical rigor toolbox maintained: Mann-Whitney U, Kruskal-Wallis, Cohen's d, shuffled null columns all appropriately applied.
success_patterns: Negative result reporting: three falsified hypotheses (H1 direction, H3 ASI, H4 phase transition) each reported with exact pre-registered target vs. actual result — model scientific writing practice.
Statistical rigor: bootstrap CIs (10k resamples), permutation null z-scores, DeLong test comparisons, Cohen's d effect sizes all appropriately applied.
GPT-2 Small as validation anchor: open model with exact Chanin labels provides cleanest, most reproducible evidence.
Audit system (79 claims, 0 discrepancies in report): rigorous claim-evidence linkage demonstrates scientific integrity.
Cross-directional metric cos(ê_p, d_c) AUROC=0.730 with z=6.38 is the paper's strongest empirical result and is correctly presented as empirical discovery.
systemic_patterns: BLOCKING issues from action_plan.json are not enforced before the writing stage — same critical numerical errors recur across iterations.

Theoretical framework contribution (Proposition 1) is persistently framed as empirically predictive despite H1 falsification, suggesting writing prompts do not adequately check theory-evidence alignment.
Cross-model generalization gap (GPT-2 Small only) has been identified in both iterations but not resolved — requires infrastructure decision in planning stage.
Label set proliferation (n=18, n=50, n=63, n=71) across different analysis stages creates confusion without a unifying label reconciliation step in the pipeline.

suggestions: Writing stage bottleneck: blocking issues from action_plan persist unresolved into writing, causing rework. Add pre-writing verification gate to eliminate rework loop.
Label set reconciliation: a dedicated 15-min audit task at the start of the writing stage would prevent the n_pos=50 vs n_pos=63 discrepancy from propagating.
GPU efficiency was good (all tasks completed, no failures); the iteration inefficiency is editorial not computational.
efficiency_analysis.notes: All 15 tasks completed successfully (gpu_progress.json shows 0 failures, 0 running). Most tasks recovered from pre-existing iter_001 results — actual new GPU computation was minimal. The iteration's computational budget was well-utilized.

recommended_focus: IMMEDIATELY: Fix five blocking issues before launching writing in iter_003 — W001 (contribution reordering + P1 falsification acknowledgment), W002 (label count reconciliation), W003 (shortlisting claim reframing), W005 (four numerical inconsistencies), W006 (abstract + structure).
HIGH PRIORITY: Promote EDA_norm as primary metric (W004) — statistically justified and mechanistically consistent with P2.
HIGH PRIORITY: Add experiment for cross-directional metric cos(ê_p, d_c) against exact labels (n_pos=18) — single computation that resolves main methodological label-mixing criticism.
MEDIUM PRIORITY: Add GPT-2 L10 EDA results to Table 1 using exact labels — expands scope beyond single configuration without infrastructure barriers.
SYSTEMIC: Implement pre-writing blocking gate (W015) to break the recurring-issue loop.
